title: Fetch, Response Admission, Abort Signals, Time Budgets, and Error Causes · Type Recall
summary: A delayed maintainer review reconstructs fetch, response admission, abort signals, time budgets, and error causes from revision, runtime, command, unknown input, validation result, request identity, response fixture, promise state, cache decision, output, abort, cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for injected bounded abort-aware Fetch client after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causa…
objectives: Inject a narrow fetch-compatible port and keep Request, Response, and validation policy separate from domain commands.
Classify ok, not found, other 4xx, 5xx, redirects, media type, and unexpected response before parsing domain JSON.
Compose caller cancellation with bounded timeout using AbortSignal APIs and preserve the first meaningful reason.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.text: A review TypeScript team handles case 6714 during an out-of-order Promise fault rehearsal. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to validate Fetch status, bytes, unknown JSON, composed abort, causes, and timer cleanup; browser TypeScript must remain deterministic and free of Node or network effects; then change one catch, owned record, export, location relation, or history transition without mutating prior state. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
